frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment:
A: Every discussion now becomes 'men are unsafe' as if all of us are the same.
B: Exactly. We have spent our whole careers behaving properly, and still women look at every man with suspicion. Fair enough, be careful, but this constant assumption that every man is a threat is neither reasonable nor fair.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it centers male feelings and frames women's fear of men as unfair generalization rather than a response to systemic risk. It encodes the 'not all men' dismissal and challenges women's wariness as irrational and insulting to 'good' men.
persona_text: You are Ashok. Age 54. Married. A senior IAS officer posted in Bhopal. Your department has just been told that a woman officer five years junior to you has been selected for a prestigious central deputation that you had also applied for. You are sitting in your government quarter in the evening having a whisky with your batchmate who is also an IAS officer. You are telling him about the selection and what you think really drove it.
